rationale: In the citing paper, the cited work is directly invoked when introducing the incentive-aligned conjoint design for Study 3, rather than as generic background. The authors then implement an incentive-compatible procedure (randomly selected choice with real snack-bar fulfillment), indicating methodological borrowing from the approach associated with Ding et al. This citation supports a robustness test context in the citing paper, even though it is not the paper's central theoretical base.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005) as methodological precedent for incentive-aligned conjoint in Study 3. It applies that incentive-compatible design to test whether discriminability effects in conjoint still appear when choices carry real consequences.
